rows.llm_rationale: Crowd price of 9.5% appears slightly underpriced given base rates for emergency cuts (~1-2 per decade) and current economic uncertainty. With ~19 months to expiry, tail risks (recession, financial crisis, geopolitical shock) justify modest premium above crowd estimate, but not dramatic divergence.
rows.question: Fed emergency rate cut before 2027?

rows.llm_rationale: Current date is May 2026, leaving ~7 months until year-end. CME FedWatch data and recent Fed communications suggest rate cuts are unlikely given inflation persistence and labor market resilience. The 15.5% crowd price appears slightly undervalued given baseline probability of at least one cut by year-end, but not by much—Fed remains hawkish. Modest upward adjustment to 18% reflects small-but-real tail risk of economic deterioration prompting emergency cuts.
rows.question: Will 1 Fed rate cut happen in 2026?
